meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no version control (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills: Fix Version Control Issues for Students
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code skills to eliminate version control bottlenecks. A high-level guide for students to build professional, scalable AI applications.
content.answer_block: To solve version control instability, integrate Claude Code with a robust Git workflow. Use Antigravity to automate repository initialization and commit hygiene. By mapping Claude Code’s agentic capabilities to your local Git hooks, you ensure every AI-generated code change is validated, tracked, and ready for deployment without manual oversight.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen filled with 'index_final_v2_real.js'—the hallmark of a developer who has lost control of their workspace. The anxiety of overwriting a breakthrough feature is the silent killer of student innovation. You are building the future, but your workflow is stuck in the past, vulnerable to the chaos of unmanaged code.

It is time to elevate your engineering standard. By mastering the synergy between Antigravity’s automation and Claude Code’s reasoning, you transition from a 'coder' to an 'architect.' This guide isn't just about saving files; it's about building a bulletproof infrastructure that turns your experimental projects into production-ready portfolio pieces.

steps.title: Initialize Repository Intelligence
steps.description: Establish a clean Git foundation before Claude touches your filesystem to ensure every AI action is traceable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project directory and generate a .gitignore file optimized for Node.js and Python, excluding all sensitive environment variables and build artifacts.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m "Initial commit: Project scaffold"

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Context
steps.description: Set up your environment variables so Claude understands your project structure and repository constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a .claude_code_config file that enforces strict file-naming conventions and requires a descriptive summary for every automated commit.

steps.title: Automate Branching Strategy
steps.description: Use Claude to manage feature branches, ensuring that experiments never pollute your main production codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the current feature task and propose a branch name following the conventional commits specification.

steps.title: Execute Atomic Commits
steps.description: Ensure every AI-generated change is a single, logical unit of work, making debugging trivial.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the recent changes in the current branch and generate a structured commit message summarizing the technical implementation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message "feat: implement authentication flow using JWT"

steps.title: Implement Automated Verification
steps.description: Run tests before merging to confirm that the AI's code hasn't introduced regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify the test suite for the modified files and execute a dry-run to ensure 100% test coverage before the merge.

pro_tips: Use 'git rerere' to teach Git how to resolve repetitive merge conflicts caused by AI-generated refactoring.
Always set your Claude Code 'max_tokens' to lower values during complex refactors to force more frequent, bite-sized commits.
Create an 'ai-audit' branch where you let Claude perform experimental refactors, keeping your main branch pristine.
Use Antigravity to generate custom git hooks that automatically trigger 'claude --review' on every pre-push.
Never allow AI to edit files outside of the current feature scope; use path-filtering to restrict its write access.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once your environment is stable, begin documenting your build process. Use the commit history generated by Claude Code to create your first README.md 'Changelog' section to impress recruiters.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the professional-grade workflow that separates hobbyists from high-impact engineers. Don't let your code drift into technical debt. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
